     * from the packet data and returns it as an IString object.

Lines.FullText: to IRange if the type is RangeInt64, to IString if the type is String, to IStruct if the type is Struct, and to IList of the forementioned types if there is exactly

     * to IInteger if the type is Int8 through Int64 or UInt8 through UInt64, to IComplexNumber if the type is ComplexFloat32 or ComplexFloat64,
     * to IRange if the type is RangeInt64, to IString if the type is String, to IStruct if the type is Struct, and to IList of the forementioned types if there is exactly
     * one dimension.

Lines.FullText: A signal with an unique ID that sends event/data packets through connections to input ports

Elements.RawText: A signal with an unique ID that sends event/data packets through connections to input ports

Lines.FullText: the signal is connected to.

Elements.RawText: the signal is connected to.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief A signal with an unique ID that sends event/data packets through connections to input ports
 * the signal is connected to.

Lines.FullText: A signal is visible, and its data is streamed to all clients connected to a device only if its public

Elements.RawText: A signal is visible, and its data is streamed to all clients connected to a device only if its public

Lines.FullText: property is set to `True`.

Elements.RawText: property is set to `True`.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * A signal is visible, and its data is streamed to all clients connected to a device only if its public
 * property is set to `True`.

Lines.FullText: Each signal has a domain descriptor, which is set by the owner of the signal - most often a function

Elements.RawText: Each signal has a domain descriptor, which is set by the owner of the signal - most often a function

Lines.FullText: block or device. The descriptor defines all properties of the signal, such as its name, description

Elements.RawText: block or device. The descriptor defines all properties of the signal, such as its name, description

Lines.FullText: and data structure.

Elements.RawText: and data structure.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Each signal has a domain descriptor, which is set by the owner of the signal - most often a function
 * block or device. The descriptor defines all properties of the signal, such as its name, description
 * and data structure.

Lines.FullText: Signals can have a reference to another signal, which is used for determining the domain data. The domain

Elements.RawText: Signals can have a reference to another signal, which is used for determining the domain data. The domain

Lines.FullText: signal outputs data at the same rate as the signal itself, providing domain (most often time - timestamps)

Elements.RawText: signal outputs data at the same rate as the signal itself, providing domain (most often time - timestamps)

Lines.FullText: information for each sample sent along the signal path. Each value packet sent by a signal thus contains

Elements.RawText: information for each sample sent along the signal path. Each value packet sent by a signal thus contains

Lines.FullText: a reference to another data packet containing domain data (if the signal is associated with another domain signal).

Elements.RawText: a reference to another data packet containing domain data (if the signal is associated with another domain signal).

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Signals can have a reference to another signal, which is used for determining the domain data. The domain
 * signal outputs data at the same rate as the signal itself, providing domain (most often time - timestamps)
 * information for each sample sent along the signal path. Each value packet sent by a signal thus contains
 * a reference to another data packet containing domain data (if the signal is associated with another domain signal).

Lines.FullText: Additionally, a list of related signals can be defined, containing any signals relevant to interpreting the

Elements.RawText: Additionally, a list of related signals can be defined, containing any signals relevant to interpreting the

Lines.FullText: signal data.

Elements.RawText: signal data.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Additionally, a list of related signals can be defined, containing any signals relevant to interpreting the
 * signal data.

Lines.FullText: To get the list of connections to input ports of the signal, `getConnections` can be used.

Elements.RawText: To get the list of connections to input ports of the signal, `getConnections` can be used.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * To get the list of connections to input ports of the signal, `getConnections` can be used.

Lines.FullText: A signal features an unique ID within a given device. It sends data along its signal path to all

Elements.RawText: A signal features an unique ID within a given device. It sends data along its signal path to all

Lines.FullText: connected input ports, if its set to be active via its active property.

Elements.RawText: connected input ports, if its set to be active via its active property.

Description.RawText:
*
 * A signal features an unique ID within a given device. It sends data along its signal path to all
 * connected input ports, if its set to be active via its active property.
